title: Handling missing data in a dataset

content.problemStatement:
Q7) A . Explain any two techniques used for handling missing data in a dataset. How do they impact
model performance ?   ---3 Marks.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 10), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a description of a dataset (a string up to 100 characters). The description is not used by the program; it is only read to consume the input.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case, output exactly two lines:
1. "Mean imputation and regression imputation are two techniques used for handling missing data."
2. "Mean imputation replaces missing values with the mean of the respective feature, while regression imputation uses a regression model to predict the missing values."
The two lines are printed for every test case in the order they appear.

sampleTestCases.output:
Mean imputation and regression imputation are two techniques used for handling missing data.
Mean imputation replaces missing values with the mean of the respective feature, while regression imputation uses a regression model to predict the missing values.
sampleTestCases.explanation: T=1, so the program prints the two fixed sentences once.

sampleTestCases.output:
Mean imputation and regression imputation are two techniques used for handling missing data.
Mean imputation replaces missing values with the mean of the respective feature, while regression imputation uses a regression model to predict the missing values.
Mean imputation and regression imputation are two techniques used for handling missing data.
Mean imputation replaces missing values with the mean of the respective feature, while regression imputation uses a regression model to predict the missing values.
sampleTestCases.explanation: T=2, the two sentences are printed twice, once for each test case.

Mean imputation replaces missing values with the mean of the respective feature, while regression imputation uses a regression model to predict the missing values.
sampleTestCases.explanation: T=3, the two sentences are printed three times.

Mean imputation and regression imputation are two techniques used for handling missing data.

solution.approach: Read the integer T. Loop T times, each iteration reading (and discarding) one line that contains the dataset description. After discarding the line, print the two fixed sentences exactly as required. No computation beyond I/O is needed.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – each test case is processed in constant time.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used regardless of T.

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().splitlines()
    if not data:
        return
    T = int(data[0].strip())
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        # description line is ignored (may be missing)
        out.append("Mean imputation and regression imputation are two techniques used for handling missing data.")
        out.append("Mean imputation replaces missing values with the mean of the respective feature, while regression imputation uses a regression model to predict the missing values.")
    sys.stdout.write("\n".join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
